auditReport.title: NO_GO: provider and ledger boundary closure

followUpAudit.title: NO_GO: public provider normalizer input boundary

baseline.head: 08516734f02280bb29c9ec374273ba3ec4236e56
baseline.interpretation: The reviewed head passes its existing suite; this matrix requires new hostile closure evidence.

findings.positiveControl: primitive hashes, CIK, identifiers, and dense symbol strings derive the pinned identities
findings.hostileRegression: coercible array/number/boolean/object hash, CIK, and symbol values reject before regex or hashing
findings.exactAndOneOver: each primitive field accepts its documented maximum and rejects one UTF-8 byte over

findings.positiveControl: a dense inert bundle validates without invoking caller behavior
findings.hostileRegression: non-array, sparse/custom-prototype arrays, inherited/accessor/symbol/cyclic/proxy inputs reject without raw runtime errors
findings.exactAndOneOver: bundle bytes, nodes, keys, array items, and depth accept exact limits and reject one-over before canonicalization

findings.positiveControl: verified-bound accepts a non-negative safe integer; all other synchronization modes accept only null
findings.hostileRegression: negative, float, string, boolean, object, and array maximumErrorMs values reject for every synchronization mode
findings.exactAndOneOver: maximumErrorMs accepts 0 and Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER, rejects -1 and unsafe-integer one-over

findings.positiveControl: one RSS-item proof and one release-visible proof recomputed from verified parents emit
findings.hostileRegression: duplicate, missing, substituted parent/policy/hash/size proof roles quarantine atomically
findings.exactAndOneOver: exactly two unique required roles accept; one missing or one additional proof rejects

findings.positiveControl: Gregorian-valid naive timestamps emit null/unknown without retrieval-time fallback
findings.hostileRegression: invalid naive date, clock, and leap-day values quarantine
findings.exactAndOneOver: timestamp field accepts 128 bytes and rejects 129 bytes through real FMP input

findings.positiveControl: actual RSS and visible-release projections at 4 MiB emit before projection hashing
findings.hostileRegression: actual RSS and visible-release projection inputs at 4 MiB + 1 quarantine before hashes/candidate output

findings.positiveControl: each documented parser, loader, manifest, proof, projection, bundle, transcript, and URL rule reaches its real public path
findings.hostileRegression: one-over and malformed URL/proof/parser inputs return no partial candidate, draft, or transcript output
findings.exactAndOneOver: every documented numeric limit has a real exact and one-over vector

findings.positiveControl: emitted fixture contains exactly one recomputed selected-item proof; terminal outcomes follow the documented proof policy
findings.hostileRegression: quarantined malformed fixture with invented proof rejects before transcript formation
findings.exactAndOneOver: one emitted proof accepts; zero/two emitted proofs and one terminal fabricated proof reject

findings.positiveControl: valid Uint8Array members within individual and aggregate limits normalize deterministically
findings.hostileRegression: null/object/string/oversized RSS or HTML members quarantine before digesting or parsing
findings.exactAndOneOver: per-member and aggregate byte ceilings accept exact bytes and reject one-over through the real normalizer

findings.positiveControl: ASCII whitespace canonicalizes as ADR 0008 specifies
findings.hostileRegression: NBSP and other Unicode whitespace remain semantically distinct from ASCII whitespace
findings.exactAndOneOver: semantic text accepts documented byte maximum and rejects one byte over without Unicode coercion

findings.positiveControl: every fact kind and parent transition, including redelivery, validates at exact dimensions
findings.hostileRegression: cross-execution parents and missing/extra clock-basis parents reject with stable observation reasons
findings.exactAndOneOver: 64 MiB/64 MiB+1 bundle bytes, 256/257 timestamp bytes, and all declared resource dimensions are executable

findings.positiveControl: ordinary exact plain inputs emit byte-for-byte identically after a one-time detached byte snapshot
findings.hostileRegression: outer and nested active or revoked accessor/proxy/inherited/symbol/sparse/custom-prototype containers execute zero traps and emit no candidate, draft, transcript identity, or partial result
findings.exactAndOneOver: FMP 10 MiB and NVIDIA 10 MiB-per-member/20 MiB-bundle byte ceilings retain real exact and one-over coverage after copying
